title: Quantize against an explicit quality budget
description: Choose a quantization scheme by measuring the accuracy delta against full precision rather than assuming int4 is free.
body: Use when shrinking a model for cheaper or faster inference. Do not assume int4 or int8 preserves quality — aggressive quantization can degrade reasoning and long-context behavior unevenly. Actions: set an acceptable quality delta, evaluate fp16/bf16/int8/int4 on the task and capability evals, and pick the smallest scheme inside the budget; prefer calibrated or QLoRA-style approaches for low bit-widths. Validation signals include a benchmark comparing each precision against full precision and a chosen scheme inside the stated delta. Risk boundary: an over-quantized model fails silently on hard inputs; if no scheme meets the budget, escalate rather than shipping degraded quality.
